payload.objective: Review the generated inbox runner together with its reviewed delegate script from the local inbox pipeline and provide a structured verdict using one of: pass, fail, needs_revision. Always output a review markdown artifact and include verdict in metadata.

params.title: BL-20260324-014 live preview smoke sample 2026-03-24 (best-effort reviewable attempt)
params.description:
Purpose:

  Controlled Trello live preview smoke for openclaw-team.

‌

  Expected behavior:

- read-only Trello ingest

- preview creation smoke only

- no business execution claim

- no Trello writeback expected in this step

‌

  Traceability:

- backlog: BL-20260324-014

- blocker context: BL-20260324-015

- created_by: Oscarling

- created_at: 2026-03-24 Asia/Shanghai

‌

  Note:

  This card is only for governed smoke verification and should remain open until the smoke is finished.

Execution contract: treat this as a best-effort, evidence-backed PDF extraction/conversion attempt. Do not claim OCR success without evidence. If full OCR/Excel conversion is not honestly achievable, return reviewable intermediate artifacts, explicit limitations, and next-step guidance. Keep behavior deterministic and limited to declared local artifacts.

review_scope.goal: Audit the wrapper and the reviewed delegate together so the review evidence can speak to the end-to-end readonly smoke path.

artifact_snapshots.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Best-effort inbox wrapper for reviewed PDF-to-Excel conversion.

This wrapper prefers delegating to the reviewed repository script
`artifacts/scripts/pdf_to_excel_ocr.py` and emits a structured JSON summary to
stdout for reviewability.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import os
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

DEFAULT_INPUT_DIR = "~/Desktop/pdf样本"
DEFAULT_OUTPUT_XLSX = "artifacts/outputs/trello_readonly/pdf_to_excel_from_trello.xlsx"
DEFAULT_OCR = "auto"
DEFAULT_DRY_RUN = False
DEFAULT_ORIGIN_ID = "trello:69c24cd3c1a2359ddd7a1bf8"
DEFAULT_TITLE = "BL-20260324-014 live preview smoke sample 2026-03-24 (best-effort reviewable attempt)"
DEFAULT_DESCRIPTION = (
    "Purpose:\n"
    "Controlled Trello live preview smoke for openclaw-team.\n"
    "Expected behavior:\n"
    "- read-only Trello ingest\n"
    "- preview creation smoke only\n"
    "- no business execution claim\n"
    "- no Trello writeback expected in this step\n"
    "Traceability:\n"
    "- backlog: BL-20260324-014\n"
    "- blocker context: BL-20260324-015\n"
    "- created_by: Oscarling\n"
    "- created_at: 2026-03-24 Asia/Shanghai\n"
    "Note:\n"
    "This card is only for governed smoke verification and should remain open until the smoke is finished."
)
DEFAULT_LABELS = ["best_effort", "evidence_backed", "readonly", "reviewable", "trello"]
DEFAULT_PREFERRED_BASE = "artifacts/scripts/pdf_to_excel_ocr.py"
DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 900


@dataclass
class Paths:
    repo_root: Path
    script_path: Path
    delegate_path: Path


def _expand_user_path(raw: str) -> Path:
    return Path(os.path.expanduser(raw)).resolve()


def _repo_root_from_script(script_path: Path) -> Path:
    return script_path.resolve().parents[2]


def _resolve_delegate(script_path: Path, preferred_base_script: str) -> Path:
    candidate = Path(preferred_base_script)
    if candidate.is_absolute():
        return candidate
    repo_root = _repo_root_from_script(script_path)
    repo_candidate = (repo_root / candidate).resolve()
    if repo_candidate.exists():
        return repo_candidate
    return (script_path.resolve().parent / candidate).resolve()


def _discover_pdfs(input_dir: Path) -> list[Path]:
    if not input_dir.exists() or not input_dir.is_dir():
        return []
    return sorted(
        p.resolve()
        for p in input_dir.rglob("*")
        if p.is_file() and p.suffix.lower() == ".pdf"
    )


def _ensure_output_parent(output_xlsx: Path) -> None:
    output_xlsx.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)


def _parse_json_text(text: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
    text = (text or "").strip()
    if not text:
        return None
    try:
        data = json.loads(text)
    except json.JSONDecodeError:
        return None
    return data if isinstance(data, dict) else None


def _load_delegate_report(stdout_text: str, report_path: Path | None) -> tuple[dict[str, Any] | None, str]:
    stdout_report = _parse_json_text(stdout_text)
    if stdout_report is not None:
        return stdout_report, "stdout"
    if report_path and report_path.exists():
        try:
            sidecar = _parse_json_text(report_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
        except OSError:
            sidecar = None
        if sidecar is not None:
            return sidecar, "sidecar"
    return None, "none"


def _status_counter(report: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, int]:
    raw = report.get("status_counter")
    if not isinstance(raw, dict):
        return {}
    result: dict[str, int] = {}
    for key, value in raw.items():
        try:
            result[str(key)] = int(value)
        except (TypeError, ValueError):
            continue
    return result


def _is_truthy_bool(value: Any) -> bool:
    return value is True


def _to_int(value: Any, default: int = 0) -> int:
    try:
        return int(value)
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        return default


def _success_evidence_ok(report: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[bool, list[str]]:
    reasons: list[str] = []
    status = report.get("status")
    total_files = _to_int(report.get("total_files"), default=-1)
    dry_run = report.get("dry_run")
    counter = _status_counter(report)
    failed_count = _to_int(counter.get("failed"), default=0)
    partial_count = _to_int(counter.get("partial"), default=0)
    excel_written = _is_truthy_bool(report.get("excel_written"))
    output_exists = _is_truthy_bool(report.get("output_exists"))
    output_size_bytes = _to_int(report.get("output_size_bytes"), default=0)

    if status != "success":
        reasons.append(f"delegate status is {status!r}, not 'success'")
    if total_files < 1:
        reasons.append(f"delegate total_files is {total_files}, expected >= 1")
    if dry_run is not False:
        reasons.append("delegate dry_run is not false")
    if failed_count != 0:
        reasons.append(f"delegate status_counter.failed is {failed_count}, expected 0")
    if partial_count != 0:
        reasons.append(f"delegate status_counter.partial is {partial_count}, expected 0")
    if not excel_written:
        reasons.append("delegate excel_written is not true")
    if not output_exists:
        reasons.append("delegate output_exists is not true")
    if output_size_bytes <= 0:
        reasons.append(f"delegate output_size_bytes is {output_size_bytes}, expected > 0")

    return (len(reasons) == 0), reasons


def _build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Reviewed inbox wrapper for PDF-to-Excel OCR helper flow")
    parser.add_argument("--input-dir", default=DEFAULT_INPUT_DIR, help="Directory containing PDF files")
    parser.add_argument("--output-xlsx", default=DEFAULT_OUTPUT_XLSX, help="Destination XLSX path")
    parser.add_argument("--ocr", default=DEFAULT_OCR, help="OCR mode to forward to delegate")
    parser.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true", default=DEFAULT_DRY_RUN, help="Short-circuit with reviewable partial outcome")
    parser.add_argument("--origin-id", default=DEFAULT_ORIGIN_ID)
    parser.add_argument("--title", default=DEFAULT_TITLE)
    parser.add_argument("--description", default=DEFAULT_DESCRIPTION)
    parser.add_argument("--label", dest="labels", action="append", default=None, help="Repeatable label")
    parser.add_argument("--preferred-base-script", default=DEFAULT_PREFERRED_BASE)
    parser.add_argument("--timeout-seconds", type=int, default=DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)
    return parser


def main() -> int:
    parser = _build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args()

    script_path = Path(__file__).resolve()
    repo_root = _repo_root_from_script(script_path)
    delegate_path = _resolve_delegate(script_path, args.preferred_base_script)
    paths = Paths(repo_root=repo_root, script_path=script_path, delegate_path=delegate_path)

    labels = args.labels if args.labels is not None else list(DEFAULT_LABELS)
    input_dir = _expand_user_path(args.input_dir)
    output_xlsx = _expand_user_path(args.output_xlsx) if Path(args.output_xlsx).is_absolute() else (repo_root / args.output_xlsx).resolve()

    pdfs = _discover_pdfs(input_dir)

    result: dict[str, Any] = {
        "status": "failed",
        "title": args.title,
        "origin_id": args.origin_id,
        "description": args.description,
        "labels": labels,
        "input": {
            "input_dir": str(input_dir),
            "output_xlsx": str(output_xlsx),
            "ocr": args.ocr,
            "dry_run": bool(args.dry_run),
        },
        "traceability": {
            "preferred_base_script": args.preferred_base_script,
            "resolved_delegate_path": str(paths.delegate_path),
            "wrapper_script": str(paths.script_path),
            "repo_root": str(paths.repo_root),
        },
        "preflight": {
            "pdf_count": len(pdfs),
            "pdf_files": [str(p) for p in pdfs],
        },
        "execution": {
            "delegated": False,
            "timeout_seconds": int(args.timeout_seconds),
        },
        "delegate": {
            "report_source": "none",
        },
        "limitations": [],
        "next_steps": [],
    }

    if output_xlsx.suffix.lower() != ".xlsx":
        result["status"] = "failed"
        result["limitations"].append("Requested output path does not end with .xlsx; refusing mismatched workbook semantics.")
        print(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
        return 1

    if not delegate_path.exists():
        result["status"] = "failed"
        result["limitations"].append("Reviewed delegate script was not found; readonly reviewable flow refuses arbitrary helper substitution.")
        result["next_steps"].append("Restore artifacts/scripts/pdf_to_excel_ocr.py or pass a valid reviewed delegate path.")
        print(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
        return 1

    if args.dry_run:
        result["status"] = "partial"
        result["limitations"].append("Dry-run requested; wrapper intentionally short-circuited before delegate execution.")
        result["next_steps"].append("Run again without --dry-run to attempt reviewed PDF-to-Excel conversion.")
        print(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
        return 0

    if len(pdfs) == 0:
        result["status"] = "partial"
        result["limitations"].append("No PDF files discovered under the provided input directory.")
        result["next_steps"].append("Place one or more .pdf files in the input directory and rerun the wrapper.")
        print(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
        return 0

    _ensure_output_parent(output_xlsx)

    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(prefix="pdf_to_excel_delegate_report_", suffix=".json", delete=False) as tmp:
        report_path = Path(tmp.name)

    cmd = [
        sys.executable,
        str(delegate_path),
        "--input-dir",
        str(input_dir),
        "--output-xlsx",
        str(output_xlsx),
        "--ocr",
        str(args.ocr),
        "--report-json",
        str(report_path),
    ]

    result["execution"]["delegated"] = True
    result["execution"]["command"] = cmd

    try:
        completed = subprocess.run(
            cmd,
            cwd=str(repo_root),
            text=True,
            capture_output=True,
            timeout=max(1, int(args.timeout_seconds)),
            check=False,
        )
    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired as exc:
        result["status"] = "failed"
        result["execution"]["timed_out"] = True
        result["execution"]["timeout_seconds"] = int(args.timeout_seconds)
        result["execution"]["stdout"] = exc.stdout
        result["execution"]["stderr"] = exc.stderr
        result["limitations"].append("Delegate execution exceeded the explicit timeout.")
        result["next_steps"].append("Retry with a higher timeout or reduce input complexity after reviewing the delegate behavior.")
        print(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
        return 1

    delegate_report, report_source = _load_delegate_report(completed.stdout, report_path)
    result["execution"]["exit_code"] = int(completed.returncode)
    result["execution"]["stdout"] = completed.stdout
    result["execution"]["stderr"] = completed.stderr
    result["delegate"]["report_source"] = report_source
    if delegate_report is not None:
        result["delegate"]["report"] = delegate_report

    if delegate_report is None:
        result["status"] = "failed"
        result["limitations"].append("Delegate did not provide a parseable structured JSON report on stdout or sidecar.")
        result["next_steps"].append("Review delegate CLI compatibility, especially --report-json support and stdout report emission.")
        print(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
        return 1

    delegate_status = delegate_report.get("status")
    if delegate_status == "partial":
        result["status"] = "partial"
        result["limitations"].append("Delegate reported a partial outcome; wrapper preserves this readonly best-effort limitation honestly.")
        result["next_steps"].append("Inspect delegate report details and input PDFs, then rerun after addressing the reported limitations.")
        print(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
        return 0

    if delegate_status != "success":
        result["status"] = "failed"
        result["limitations"].append(f"Delegate reported non-success status: {delegate_status!r}.")
        result["next_steps"].append("Inspect the delegate report and stderr for the reviewed failure cause.")
        print(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
        return 1

    ok, reasons = _success_evidence_ok(delegate_report)
    if not ok:
        result["status"] = "failed"
        result["limitations"].append("Delegate reported success, but strong success attestation gates were not satisfied.")
        result["limitations"].extend(reasons)
        result["next_steps"].append("Review delegate report contract fields and rerun once the reviewed script emits complete success evidence.")
        print(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
        return 1

    result["status"] = "success"
    result["output"] = {
        "output_xlsx": str(output_xlsx),
        "excel_written": True,
        "output_exists": True,
        "output_size_bytes": _to_int(delegate_report.get("output_size_bytes"), default=0),
    }
    print(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

review_contract.artifact_policy: Always include either `file_contents` for the review artifact path or `artifacts` referencing that exact path.
review_contract.fallback_policy: If evidence is insufficient, return `status=partial`, include explicit `errors`, still generate review artifact content, and set verdict=needs_revision.

constraints: Review must be grounded in produced automation artifact
When both wrapper and reviewed delegate snapshots are supplied, evaluate them as one end-to-end readonly pair instead of ignoring the delegate evidence.
Do not invent missing artifact content
Return a clear verdict: pass, fail, or needs_revision
Generate review artifact markdown for expected_outputs[0].path
Use artifact_snapshots when provided; avoid claiming access problems if snapshot content exists.
When both the generated wrapper and reviewed delegate snapshots are provided, review them together rather than silently narrowing scope to one file.
Return metadata.verdict with one of: pass, fail, needs_revision.
Always generate review artifact content for expected_outputs[0].path.
If evidence is insufficient, use partial + errors + verdict=needs_revision, but still output review artifact.

source.title: BL-20260324-014 live preview smoke sample 2026-03-24 (best-effort reviewable attempt)

acceptance_criteria: Produce a review artifact with explicit verdict (pass/fail/needs_revision)

result.summary: Reviewed the inbox runner and paired delegate together as an end-to-end readonly smoke path. Verdict: needs_revision. The wrapper is well-aligned with the governed best-effort/read-only review contract and adds strong evidence gating, but the delegate has a material bug: OCR-only successful extraction can still be marked failed because status is derived from the presence of any OCR error even when text was extracted. This undermines truthful end-to-end outcomes for the wrapper/delegate pair.
